JK: other methods of decomposition




in terms of the calculations we’re going to show them, the idea of having sub-population rates only really fits into the picture when we introduce cross classified stuff? otherwise it just causes more of an issue by having to have a sum at the start of everything.

it’s quite hard to come up with a rate that is the product of 2 or 3 factors that isn’t sub-populations. DG tends to use birth rate related things, because they essentially go down through a series: women>sexuallyactive>pregnant>birthed. could we use something (maybe SCJS) like victimofcrime>victimofpropertycrime>victimofburglary ?

somewhere we should talk about the unit of analysis being populations.

i’m not quite sure what i mean by that really.
consider 2 pops: uk, italy. death rate from covid is higher in italy because higher proportion of over 70s.

all of this std/decomp stuff doesn’t allow you to say:

if an italian was british, the prob of them dying would be XX.

instead it is:

if italy was like britain, the rate of deaths would be XX

maybe that’s obvious though. it feels more blurred when it’s in the oaxaca approach for some reason.

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
intro lec
I have two populations \(p1\) and \(p2\)
Standardisation
\[
\text{What would }R_{p2}\text{ be if }A\text{ were the same in }p1\text{ and }p2
\]
Decomposition
\[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }A\text{ being different between }p2\text{ and }p1?
\] or \[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }p1\text{ and }p2\text{ having different amounts of }A?
\]

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
intro lec 2
I have two populations \(p1\) and \(p2\)
Standardisation
\[
\text{What would }R_{p2}\text{ be if }\left\{\begin{array}{lr}
        A\text{ were what it is in }p2 \\
        A\text{ were at the average of what it is in }p1\text{ and }p2 \\
        \end{array}\right\}
\]
Decomposition
\[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }A\text{ being different between }p2\text{ and }p1?
\] \[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }p1\text{ and }p2\text{ having different amounts of }A?
\]

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
later lec
\[
\text{What would }\bar{Y}_{p2}\text{ be if }\left\{\begin{array}{lr}
        \bar A_{p2}\text{ were }\bar A_{p1} \\
        \bar A_{p2}\text{ were }\frac{\bar A_{p1}+\bar A_{p2}}{2} \\
        \end{array}\right\}
\]
\[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }A\text{ being different between }p2\text{ and }p1?
\]
\[
\text{How much of }R_{p2} - R_{p1}\text{ is because of }p1\text{ and }p2\text{ having different amounts of }A?
\] \[
\text{How much of }\bar Y_{p2} - \bar Y_{p1}\text{ is because of }\bar A_{p2} - \bar A_{p1}?
\]

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
Decomposition - {DasGuptR}
2 factor



library(tibble)

mydat <- tribble(
  ~pop,~psq,~pgr,
  "P1", 5/15, 3/5,
  "P2", 10/15, 8/10,
) 
mydat

# A tibble: 2 × 3
  pop     psq   pgr
  <chr> <dbl> <dbl>
1 P1    0.333   0.6
2 P2    0.667   0.8




library(DasGuptR)

dgres <- dgnpop(x = mydat,
       pop = "pop",
       factors = c("psq","pgr"))

head(dgres)

  rate pop std.set factor
1 0.20  P1    <NA>  crude
2 0.53  P2    <NA>  crude
3 0.23  P1      P2    psq
4 0.47  P2      P1    psq
5 0.30  P1      P2    pgr
6 0.40  P2      P1    pgr

dg_table(dgres)

        P1   P2 diff decomp
psq   0.23 0.47 0.23     70
pgr   0.30 0.40 0.10     30
crude 0.20 0.53 0.33    100

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
Decomposition - {DasGuptR}
3 factor



library(tibble)

mydat <- tribble(
  ~pop,~pcol,~psq,~pgr,
  "P1", 11/15, 4/11, 3/4,
  "P2", 13/15, 10/13, 8/10,
) 
mydat

# A tibble: 2 × 4
  pop    pcol   psq   pgr
  <chr> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
1 P1    0.733 0.364  0.75
2 P2    0.867 0.769  0.8 




library(DasGuptR)

dgres <- dgnpop(x = mydat,
       pop = "pop",
       factors = c("pcol","psq","pgr"))

head(dgres)

  rate pop std.set factor
1 0.20  P1    <NA>  crude
2 0.53  P2    <NA>  crude
3 0.32  P1      P2   pcol
4 0.38  P2      P1   pcol
5 0.23  P1      P2    psq
6 0.48  P2      P1    psq

dg_table(dgres)

        P1   P2  diff decomp
pcol  0.32 0.38 0.059   17.6
psq   0.23 0.48 0.252   75.5
pgr   0.34 0.37 0.023    6.9
crude 0.20 0.53 0.333  100.0

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
sub-populations



mydat <- tribble(
  ~pop, ~shape,    ~pshp, ~pgreen,
  1,    "circle",  10/24,  2/10,
  1,    "square",  10/24,  3/10,
  1,    "triangle", 4/24,  3/4,
  2,    "circle",   7/24,  2/7,
  2,    "square",   7/24,  4/7,
  2,    "triangle",10/24,  8/10,
)
mydat

# A tibble: 6 × 4
    pop shape     pshp pgreen
  <dbl> <chr>    <dbl>  <dbl>
1     1 circle   0.417  0.2  
2     1 square   0.417  0.3  
3     1 triangle 0.167  0.75 
4     2 circle   0.292  0.286
5     2 square   0.292  0.571
6     2 triangle 0.417  0.8  



Rate_green = sum ( prop_shape_i * prop_green_i )

title: 2 and 3 factor decomposition
text:
sub-populations

dgnpop(x = mydat,
       pop = "pop",
       factors = c("pshp","pgreen"),
       id_vars = c("shape"),
       ratefunction = "sum(pshp*pgreen)") |>
  dg_table()

          1    2 diff decomp
pshp   0.41 0.52 0.11     44
pgreen 0.40 0.54 0.14     56
crude  0.33 0.58 0.25    100
